_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-24R Fentanyl (Revised 12/10/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2026). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-24R: Fentanyl — MDHHS Approved 1/5/2026, Reviewed 2026
precautions: Monitor respiratory rate, SpO2, and mental status continuously during and after administration
IM route: single dose only — no repeat
Elderly patients and those with reduced body mass: consider lower end of dosing range
IN route: use mucosal atomizer device (MAD); split dose — half per nostril
expected_effects: Significant analgesia within 2-3 minutes IV, 5-10 minutes IM/IN
side_effects: Chest wall rigidity (with rapid high-dose IV — rare at EMS doses)
notes: IV/IO/IN: may repeat ONCE after minimum 10 minutes; maximum total 200 mcg
IM: single dose only; no repeat
Procedural sedation adult: 50-100 mcg (1 mcg/kg) IV/IO titrated slowly; IN if available; may repeat every 4 minutes to a maximum of 3 mcg/kg
Procedural sedation pediatric: per MI-MEDIC; if unavailable, 1 mcg/kg IV/IO titrated slowly, IN if available; may repeat every 5 minutes to a maximum of 3 mcg/kg
Pediatric pain management: per MI-MEDIC; if unavailable, 1 mcg/kg IV/IO/IM/IN

dosing.dose: ≤25 kg→25 mcg; 26-50 kg→50 mcg; 51-75 kg→75 mcg; ≥76 kg→100 mcg; may repeat ONCE after ≥10 min; max total 200 mcg
dosing.notes: IN: use MAD; split half per nostril; IV/IO should be titrated slowly

dosing.dose: Same weight-rounded dose; single dose only — NO repeat
50-100 mcg (1 mcg/kg) IV/IO titrated slowly; IN if available; may repeat every 4 minutes to maximum 3 mcg/kg
dosing.notes: Used for patient procedural sedation per protocol
Reference MI-MEDIC for weight-based maximum dose; IN: use MAD; split half per nostril

dosing.dose: Per MI-MEDIC; if unavailable, 1 mcg/kg IV/IO titrated slowly; may repeat every 5 minutes to maximum 3 mcg/kg
dosing.notes: Use for patient procedural sedation per protocol
